title: Based 3D Bioreactor for Effective Selection, Activation, and Transduction of T
description: This application will focus on development of a novel perfusion-based, microbead-free 3D bioreactor to achieve the selection, activation, and gene transduction of T cells in a single continuous processing system.
text:
Current T cell manufacturing methods, adapted from other medical uses, face challenges like high costs, safety concerns, and inefficiencies, especially for leukemia patients. Automation is being developed to address these issues, but its success is still uncertain.
The proposed solution involves developing a closed-loop perfusion circuit for T cell engineering and expansion using two bioreactors: a small one for selection, activation, and transduction (Stage 1), and a larger one for expansion (Stage 2).
By implementing a beads-free, closed-loop T cell manufacturing system, an organization could reduce manufacturing failures and reagent costs by eliminating reliance on magnetic bead platforms, enabling efficient, scalable, and automated ACT production that improves safety and accelerates time-to-market for cell therapies.
